violations.text: The floors in the kitchen, under the shelving, are constructed of wood which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. PIC stated flooring material is in basement and needs to be installed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2025

Food contact surfaces of can opener and deli slicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Both items were taken out of service. Proper cleaning procedures reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2025

Various foods were observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC relocated all foods because food was stocked from walk-in about 2 hours prior. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2025

The door to the outside, located in the in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Door not tight fitting due to damaged floor. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2025

The left side of the main prep unit and dressing prep unit was observed with an elevated ambient temperature. All food was observed elevated in temperature as well. Unit to not be used until serviced. PIC called technician at time of inspection. Food in unit to be checked after unit is serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2025

The floors in the kitchen, under the shelving, are constructed of wood which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2025

The following equipment was observed in poor repair... - Walk in cooler (basement) and prepared salad refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. - The handwash sink faucet located in the kitchen was observed to be leaking and spraying. Equipment must be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. Per the PIC, more time is needed for repairs to equipment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2023

At the time of inspection, an employee certified in food safety was not available/on site. The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Per the PIC, employee(s) to take Food Safety Course to ensure an employee certified in food safety is on site at all times. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2023

Employee was observed wiping gloves on dirty apron without changing gloves. Inspector requested employee to change gloves, at which point the employee did not wash hands. Food employees must wash their hands before putting on gloves. PIC informed and had employee wash hands. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2023

Sliced italian deli meats were observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of food items at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2023
Food items prepared on site were observed to be held longer than 7 days post preparation date. Items were voluntary disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2023

Food items previously prepared, frozen, and thawed were observed not marked with an expiration date of six days from the date of defrosting. PIC to ensure dates of prepared food items are adjusted for date of defrost. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2023

At the time of inspection sliced pizza was observed out on a rack out of temperature control. If time without temperature control is used as the public health control for a working supply of TIME/TEMPERATURE CONTROL FOR SAFETY FOOD before cooking, or for READY-TO-EAT TIME/TEMPERATURE CONTROL FOR SAFETY FOOD that is displayed or held for sale or service written procedures shall be prepared in advance, maintained in the FOOD ESTABLISHMENT and made available to the REGULATORY AUTHORITY upon request. PIC in charge to submit food safety plan and sent to RIDOH. Information left with PIC at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2023

The knives were stored on an unclean surface (in between prep unit surfaces, between handwash sink and prep sink) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2023
Single service items are not being stored properly and being stored on a surface that is not clean on the floor in the basement. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2023

The handwash sink faucet located in the kitchen was observed to be leaking and spraying. Equipment must be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2023
Walk in cooler (basement) and prepared salad refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2023
The condensate from the refrigeration unit is draining into the mop sink. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2023

The walls in the upstairs prep area are not covered exposing the wooden internal structure. Materials for wall surfaces/coverings shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2023
Inspector observed missing ceiling tiles in basement. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2023
Inspector observed light shields to be cracked. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2023

The smooth nonabsorbant shelf holding flour was observed to be worn and exposing wood structure. This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2023
